judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly answer all four questions with essentially equivalent depth; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: `main`. The branch `feat/pairwise-metric` was built on top of `feat/weighted-scorer`... there is no reason to target the now-obsolete `feat/weighted-scorer` branch, and doing so would produce a PR against a branch whose own work is stranded off of `main`'s history.

report_2.evidence: `main`. Per project documentation's rule "base every PR on the trunk," and since `feat/weighted-scorer` — the branch this work actually depends on — has already been merged into `main` (see Q2), there is no live intermediate branch left to stack on.

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric`... was created on top of `feat/weighted-scorer`'s tip, `ac9d505`... `feat/weighted-scorer` is **not an ancestor of `main`** (`git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` → `NO`). Meanwhile `main` moved forward on 2026-06-13 via PR #42, which squash-merged that exact `feat/weighted-scorer` content into a single commit.

report_2.evidence: was branched from `ac9d505`... the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer`... That starting point (`feat/weighted-scorer`) is **dead** — it has already been squash-merged into `main`... `git branch --contains feat/weighted-scorer` does not list `main`, confirming the original three W-commits are not literally in `main`'s history; they were merged via squash under a different commit hash, in PR #42.

report_1.evidence: Three-dot diff (`git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric`... this is what GitHub actually shows for a PR) — 21 lines across 3 files, additionally including: `scoring/scorer.py`: the entire weighted-scoring rewrite... **This weighted-scorer content is not pairwise-metric work.** It's present only because `feat/pairwise-metric` branched from `feat/weighted-scorer` before that work was (squash-)merged into `main`

report_2.evidence: **21 lines across 3 files** — `prompts/grader.txt`, `scoring/metrics.py`, and `scoring/scorer.py`. This reintroduces the entire `overlap()` function and the `WEIGHTS`-based scoring rewrite in `scorer.py`... they are dependency baggage from in-progress work that has since landed under a different commit

report_1.evidence: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`. This replays only the commits unique to `feat/pairwise-metric`... dropping `W1`/`W2`/`W3` entirely... Verify afterward with `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` — it should match today's 5-line two-dot diff, and now `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` (three-dot) should match it too, confirming the branch is truly rebased and merge-base has moved to `main`'s tip.

report_2.evidence: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` to drop the now-redundant W-commits... **Re-verify the diff is scoped correctly** after the rebase: `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric`... should show only the ~5-line pairwise addition (`pairwise_preference` in `scoring/metrics.py`, the grader-prompt line)
